api: VWO/Wingify API v2 (https://app.vwo.com/api/v2, documented at developers.wingify.com under the same https://app.wingify.com/api/v2 host family)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: researched against the live OpenAPI specs published per-endpoint at developers.wingify.com (fetched via developers.wingify.com/llms.txt's page index and each page's embedded OpenAPI 3.1 definition). CORRECTION vs. the prior wave2 version: legacy's bare GET /campaigns targets a URL shape the real, currently-documented API does not expose -- every real campaign endpoint is account-scoped (/accounts/{account_id}/campaigns...) and authenticates via an apiKey header literally named 'token' (bare value, no prefix), not the 'Authorization: Token <key>' shape legacy sent. Both are now fixed (spec.json's new account_id, defaulting to VWO's own 'current' keyword so no new REQUIRED config is introduced; streams.json's auth header). Scope is the direct Campaign-management surface (list/detail/create/update) that is the natural expansion of legacy's original single campaigns stream, PLUS the per-campaign Variations sub-resource, now implemented as a fan_out stream (campaign_variations: one preliminary campaigns list call, then one GET .../variations call per campaign id, stamping campaign_id onto every emitted variation record -- conventions.md S3's fan_out dialect, real response shape confirmed against developers.wingify.com/reference/get-all-the-variations-of-a-campaign.md). Excluded as out_of_scope (Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, a follow-up pass): the per-campaign Goals sub-resource (its own dedicated reference page could not be reached to confirm a real response shape independent of the campaigns stream's own goals summary field -- SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS pending a reachable source) and Sections (multivariate-campaigns-only; its reference page's response schema/example was not populated in the published docs, an unconfirmed shape not worth guessing), the entire unrelated Wingify developer-hub surface for other products (mobile/web SDKs, Insights/heatmap metric reports, custom widgets, FullStack feature flags, websites/workspaces administration) which is a different product family from the Campaign-management REST API this connector's identity targets, and the campaign share-link/draft-update endpoints (workspace-UI-collaboration features, not syncable campaign data).

excluded.reason: single-campaign detail; identical record shape to the campaigns list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
generates a shareable UI collaboration URL for the campaign, not syncable campaign data

bulk update of DRAFT (not-yet-launched) campaigns for the current sub-account; a distinct bulk-draft-editing workflow endpoint, not a per-record mutation this dialect's single-record write actions model; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

per-campaign goals list; the endpoint's own dedicated reference page (developers.wingify.com/reference/get-the-goals-of-a-campaign) returned 404 and is not indexed in the site's llms.txt page list, leaving no reachable source to confirm a response shape distinct from the campaigns stream's own goals summary field (partialCollection/offset/totalCount) -- SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS, not modeled without a confirmed shape
creating a goal requires an existing campaign_id from the (unreachable-shape) goals list; same SCHEMA_AMBIGUOUS reasoning as the goals list endpoint
single-goal detail; same fan-out dependency as the (unmodeled) goals list endpoint

update a campaign goal; same fan-out dependency as the goals list endpoint (note: this endpoint's own published path uses singular 'campaign', inconsistent with every sibling goals path's plural 'campaigns' -- documented here verbatim, not a transcription error introduced by this bundle)

creating a variation requires an existing campaign_id from the campaign_variations fan-out list (now covered as a read stream); modeling the corresponding write action is a follow-up pass -- the write dialect's fan-out-derived-id write shape (a create/update action whose path needs a per-record campaign_id not itself supplied by the caller's record) was not built out this pass
update a campaign variation; same follow-up-pass reasoning as the create-variation write
per-campaign sections list (MULTIVARIATE campaigns only); requires the same campaign-id fan-out as goals/variations, not built out this pass
creating a section requires an existing campaign_id from the (currently unbuilt) fan-out sections list; Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage, follow-up pass
update a campaign section; same fan-out dependency as the sections list endpoint
lists the caller's own accessible sub-accounts/workspaces (an account-selection/administration surface), not campaign data this connector's identity targets
